OF BENEFIT TO GRANNY
Concessions to Dam the Columbia

NOrthpOrt and Republic.

OF BENEFIT TO GRANNY

pages.raw_text:
Jay p. Graves, in speaking of the
contemplated power plant at Kettle
Falls. for which concessions to dam
the Columbia river were passed in
the United States senate. said that
he did not expect to live to see the
full horsepower capable of being de-
veloped made use of.


The principal object of the erection
of the plant will be the supply of
power. to the Grapby smelter, which
now pays a rental on something like
5000 horsepower.


The power plant will probably also
furnish the power for the Northport
smelter, about 25 miles distant, and
to the town of Republic, about the
same distance away.


There is fall Of about 46 feet,
which can be easily handled at the
point proposed to be damned, and it
is the company's intention to only OBJ
struct part Of the river.
